chapter: Chapter 5: The Fundamental Unit of Life

questions.question: Explain the discovery of the cell.
questions.answer: The cell was discovered by Robert Hooke in 1665 when he observed a thin slice of cork under a microscope. He noticed small box-like compartments and called them cells.

questions.question: Explain the cell theory.
questions.answer: Cell theory states that all living organisms are composed of cells, the cell is the basic unit of life, and new cells arise from pre-existing cells.

questions.question: Explain the structure and function of the plasma membrane.
questions.answer: The plasma membrane is a thin, flexible and selectively permeable membrane that surrounds the cell. It regulates the movement of substances in and out of the cell.

questions.question: Explain diffusion with an example.
questions.answer: Diffusion is the movement of substances from a region of higher concentration to lower concentration. Example: oxygen diffusing into cells.

questions.question: Explain osmosis with an example.
questions.answer: Osmosis is the movement of water across a selectively permeable membrane from high concentration to low concentration. Example: absorption of water by plant roots.

questions.question: Explain the structure and function of the cell wall.
questions.answer: The cell wall is a rigid outer layer present in plant cells made of cellulose. It provides shape, strength and protection.

questions.question: Explain the structure and function of the nucleus.
questions.answer: The nucleus is a membrane-bound organelle that contains genetic material and controls cell activities.

questions.question: Explain the functions of cytoplasm.
questions.answer: Cytoplasm is the jelly-like substance in the cell where most metabolic activities occur.

questions.question: Explain the role of mitochondria.
questions.answer: Mitochondria are called the powerhouse of the cell because they produce energy in the form of ATP through respiration.

questions.question: Explain the role of ribosomes in the cell.
questions.answer: Ribosomes are small organelles responsible for protein synthesis.

questions.question: Explain the functions of endoplasmic reticulum.
questions.answer: Endoplasmic reticulum forms a network of membranes that helps in synthesis and transport of proteins and lipids.

questions.question: Explain the role of Golgi apparatus.
questions.answer: Golgi apparatus modifies, packages and transports proteins and lipids to different parts of the cell.

questions.question: Explain the functions of lysosomes.
questions.answer: Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes that break down wastes and damaged cell parts.

questions.question: Explain plastids and their types.
questions.answer: Plastids are organelles found in plant cells. Types include chloroplasts, chromoplasts and leucoplasts.

questions.question: Explain the role of chloroplasts in plants.
questions.answer: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and help plants perform photosynthesis.

questions.question: Explain the function of vacuoles.
questions.answer: Vacuoles store water, food, wastes and help maintain cell turgidity.

questions.question: Explain the structure of chromosomes.
questions.answer: Chromosomes are thread-like structures composed of DNA and proteins present in the nucleus.

questions.question: Explain the role of DNA in cells.
questions.answer: DNA carries genetic information and controls protein synthesis.

questions.question: Explain the importance of genes.
questions.answer: Genes are units of DNA responsible for inheritance of traits.

questions.question: Explain mitosis.
questions.answer: Mitosis is cell division producing two identical daughter cells.

questions.question: Explain meiosis.
questions.answer: Meiosis is cell division producing four daughter cells with half the chromosome number.

questions.question: Explain the difference between plant and animal cells.
questions.answer: Plant cells have cell wall and chloroplasts while animal cells do not.

questions.question: Explain prokaryotic cells with examples.
questions.answer: Prokaryotic cells lack a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. Example: bacteria.

questions.question: Explain eukaryotic cells with examples.
questions.answer: Eukaryotic cells have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles. Example: plant and animal cells.

questions.question: Explain the importance of cell division.
questions.answer: Cell division is important for growth, repair of tissues and reproduction.
